claim: Google Trends weekly 'bitcoin' attention; high-attention fade / low-attention accumulate, strictly-lagged
data: stitched weekly Google Trends 'bitcoin' interest 490 wk 2017-01-01..2026-05-17 (fresh fetch, cookie-primed, 35s 429-backoff) + native monthly backbone 113 pts; BTC daily close 3197d on-disk. Strictly-lagged: weekly value applied only PUB_LAG=3d after the week closes.

key_findings.thesis_direction_wrong: level-attention-z forward IC is POSITIVE (+0.10@21d,+0.14@42d) i.e. high attention precedes HIGHER returns -- opposite of 'fade'. Even granting the best (momentum) direction, it dies.
key_findings.fade_canonical_negative: the pre-registered fade-both-legs rule is net Sharpe -0.79 IS.
key_findings.baselines: best momentum config 0.839 > B&H 0.509 and > random-lottery95 0.701 (the only gate it passes besides net-of-cost/bootstrap) -- this is the long-beta+vintage 'looks good' trap.
key_findings.deflated_sharpe: p=0.378 @N=96 (need>0.95) FAIL -- apparent Sharpe explained by best-of-96 selection.
key_findings.AR_matched_placebo_RIGHT_NULL: p=0.055 @N=1000 (placebo95=0.851 >= real 0.839). Indistinguishable from an AR(1) wiggle with matched autocorrelation riding BTC bull drift -> zero attention content.
key_findings.consume_once_holdout: OOS net Sharpe -0.250 (first-half +0.44, second-half -1.01); edge inverts out of sample.
key_findings.PIT_note: 1-week CHANGE in attention-z (the differencing that removes the rescaled trend) has forward IC ~0 (+0.017..-0.016). The only 'signal' lives in the slow level, which is exactly the vintage/rescaling + long-beta artifact the BACKLOG flagged.

binding_gate: deflated_sharpe (and independently fails cpcv_pbo, haircut, AR-placebo, holdout)
